steps.observed: OwnedAsset model found with 15 fields including userId, itemType, itemId, purchasePrice, quantity, quantityAvailable, status, referenceNumber

steps.action: Check DB schema via sqlite3 .schema OwnedAsset
steps.expected: OwnedAsset table exists with FK to User
steps.observed: Table exists with all fields and FK constraint OwnedAsset_userId_fkey
OwnedAsset is a separate model with its own enum, relations, and fields not present in Order
Listing model found with 16 fields including sellerId, itemType, itemId, askPrice, quantity, status, referenceNumber

steps.action: Check DB schema via sqlite3 .schema Listing
steps.expected: Listing table exists with FK to User
steps.observed: Table exists with all fields and FK constraints to User and OwnedAsset

steps.action: Verify separation from Order and catalog models
steps.expected: Listing is separate from Order, Product, Event
steps.observed: Listing is a dedicated model with seller relation, own enum, and no overlap with Order

steps.expected: At minimum: pending, active, sold, delivered, cancelled equivalents
steps.observed: 6 statuses: pending, confirmed, delivered, listed, sold, cancelled

steps.expected: At minimum: pending, active, sold, delivered, cancelled equivalents
steps.observed: 6 statuses: draft, active, pending_sale, sold, cancelled, expired
All required stages covered: pending (OwnedAsset), active (Listing), sold (both), delivered (OwnedAsset), cancelled (both), plus extras: confirmed, listed, draft, pending_sale, expired

assertions.title: Owned assets and listings are relationally linked

steps.observed: Listing has ownedAssetId String? with @relation(fields: [ownedAssetId], references: [id]) to OwnedAsset
OwnedAsset has listings Listing[] field
User has ownedAssets OwnedAsset[] and listings Listing[]
Listing_ownedAssetId_fkey and Listing_sellerId_fkey and OwnedAsset_userId_fkey all present

steps.expected: Some listings linked to owned assets
steps.observed: LIST-2026-00001 linked to ASSET-2026-00002, LIST-2026-00002 linked to ASSET-2026-00001
5 OwnedAsset records all belonging to test@example.com (role=user, not admin)
Quantities range from 1 to 4
Prices range from $34.99 to $299.99
5 distinct statuses: pending, confirmed, delivered, listed, sold

assertions.title: Seed data includes pseudo listings in multiple states

steps.action: Query Listing table grouped by status
steps.observed: 5 distinct statuses: active (1), cancelled (1), draft (1), pending_sale (1), sold (1)
5 Listing records with realistic prices ($35-$349.99), quantities (1-2), and item references

evidence.dbQuery: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM Listing GROUP BY status -> 5 distinct statuses

assertions.title: Legacy thin Order model is no longer the sole transaction source

steps.expected: Order exists but is marked legacy
steps.observed: Order model has comment: 'Legacy Order model - retained for backwards compatibility' and 'New pseudo transactions use OwnedAsset and Listing models'

steps.expected: New models are richer and separate
steps.observed: Order has 2-state enum (to_be_paid, paid). OwnedAsset has 6-state enum. Listing has 6-state enum. Both new models have richer fields and relations.
Schema comments explicitly state Order is legacy and new transactions use OwnedAsset/Listing. Both old and new models coexist.

summary: Tested 7 assertions: all 7 passed. The Prisma schema defines OwnedAsset and Listing as dedicated persisted models with rich lifecycle enums (6 statuses each), proper relational links (User->OwnedAsset->Listing with FK constraints), and explicit separation from the legacy Order model. Seed data includes 5 owned assets and 5 listings for the non-admin test user with realistic data across multiple lifecycle states.
